div.p:
Excellency: I have the honor to acknowledge the receipt of your note No. 153, of the 15th instant, furnishing the Spanish text of the draft of a convention submitted with my note No. 127, of the 31st
ultimo, continuing in force and effect for an indefinite period the water boundary convention of March 1, 1889, and the commission established thereunder.

I have had the two texts prepared for signature and beg to inclose for your inspection the United States and Mexican copies of the convention. If found correct, I shall be pleased to sign the convention with you to-morrow morning at 11 o’clock, if you can find it convenient to call at that hour.
